---
title: "Patterned Landscapes of the Everglades"
format:
  html:
    css: ../assets/css/custom.css
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
    title-block-banner: false
---

::: {.journey-hero .hero-shell}


::: {.hero-copy .container}


<p class="journey-meta">Everglades, Florida, USA · 2009–2015</p>

<p class="journey-reflection">“A short reflection on how this place changed the way I think.”</p>
:::
::: {.journey-hero}
![Aerial view of the Everglades ridge and slough pattern, showing alternating linear bands of darker green elevated ridges separated by lighter green water-filled sloughs stretching into the distance. The geometric striations create a rhythmic, flowing composition that emphasizes the landscape's complex hydrological structure. Image is from internet.](../assets/images/journeys/everglades/hero.jpg){.journey-hero-image}

:::
:::

::: {.section .container-fluid .journey-glance}
::: {.row .gx-4 .gy-4}
::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card .journey-card}
### Location

Everglades, Florida, USA
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card .journey-card}
### Years

2009–2015
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card .journey-card}
### Themes

Scale · Feedback · Complexity
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-6 .col-xl-3}
::: {.feature-card .journey-card}
### Related Research

A brief note or citation
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section .container .journey-story}
## The Story

under construction. This story is still being written. Please check back later for updates.
:::

::: {.section .container-fluid .journey-gallery}
::: {.row .gx-4 .gy-4}
::: {.col-12 .col-lg-6}
::: {.gallery-card}
<img src="../assets/images/journeys/everglades/ecosystem.jpg" width="1200" height="800" alt="Placeholder landscape image" />
<p class="gallery-caption"> Ridge and Slough ecosystem.</p>
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-lg-6}
::: {.gallery-card}
<img src="../assets/images/journeys/everglades/aerial.jpg" width="1200" height="800" alt="Aerial view of the Everglades ridge and slough pattern" />
<p class="gallery-caption">Aerial view of the Everglades ridge and slough pattern.</p>
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-lg-6}
::: {.gallery-card}
<img src="../assets/images/journeys/everglades/vector.jpg" width="1200" height="800" alt="Vector illustration of the Everglades ridge and slough pattern" />
<p class="gallery-caption">Vector map of the Everglades ridge and slough mosaic.</p>
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
<img src="../assets/images/journeys/everglades/people.jpg" width="1200" height="800" alt="People working in the Everglades" />
<p class="gallery-caption">Ecohydrology lab members conducting field work in the Everglades.</p>

:::

:::
:::

::: {.section .journey-thinking}
::: {.container}
## Thinking Across Scales

This is the signature section. Summarize how this place changed your understanding of systems, scale, feedback, or complexity. Keep it clear, concise, and slightly more distilled than the main narrative.
:::
:::

::: {.section .container .journey-essays}
## Related Essays

::: {.row .gx-4 .gy-4}
::: {.col-12 .col-md-4}
::: {.feature-card .essay-card}
### Essay One

A short note on how this idea connects to another piece of writing.

[Read more →](#)
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-4}
::: {.feature-card .essay-card}
### Essay Two

A short note on how this idea connects to another piece of writing.

[Read more →](#)
:::
:::

::: {.col-12 .col-md-4}
::: {.feature-card .essay-card}
### Essay Three

A short note on how this idea connects to another piece of writing.

[Read more →](#)
:::
:::
:::
:::
